name: Castillo de Jadraque — circular from Jadraque
name_original: Castillo de Jadraque — circular desde Jadraque
region_intro: Hiking around Alcarria Alta offers diverse terrain shaped by the rising Sistema Ibérico, featuring undulating strata of limestones, gypsum, red sandstone, and conglomerate stones. The region is characterized by deep valleys and gorges carved by rivers like the Tagus, Tajuña, and Badiel, alongside numerous karst phenomena. Hikers can explore a landscape that includes picturesque river canyons, emblematic hills, and extensive lavender fields, providing varied natural features for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Alcarria Alta The most popular hiking route is Peñascos de los Frailes — circular desde Las Inviernas, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of rock formations and the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pico del Alar – circular by Salinas de la Inesperada from Ocentejo – Parque Natural del Alto Tajo, a moderate 9.8 miles (15.7 km) path. This trail features significant elevation gain and leads through the Alto Tajo Natural Park. Local hikers also love the Civica and Civica waterfall - circular along the Tajuña river, a 6.5 miles (10.5 km) trail leading through river valleys and past waterfalls, often completed in about 2 hours 50 minutes. Hiking around Alcarria Alta is defined by river valleys, gorges, karst phenomena, and limestone formations. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Alcarria Alta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Alcarria Alta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Alcarria Alta?
A: Alcarria Alta offers a diverse network of nearly 40 hiking trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 9 easy, 26 moderate, and 2 difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable path for every hiker.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Alcarria Alta?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Alcarria Alta are spring and autumn. During these times, the weather is generally pleasant, and the natural landscapes, from river valleys to lavender fields, are particularly vibrant.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Alcarria Alta?
A: Yes, Alcarria Alta features several excellent circular routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027290918/penascos-de-los-frailes-circular-desde-las-inviernas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñascos de los Frailes — circular desde Las Inviernas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 11.8 km trail offering views of unique rock formations. Another popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027268114/civica-and-civica-waterfall-circular-along-the-tajuna-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Civica and Civica waterfall - circular along the Tajuña river&lt;/a&gt;, a 10.5 km route that takes you through picturesque river valleys and past waterfalls.

Q: Can I find hikes with waterfalls in Alcarria Alta?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its beautiful river systems, and you can find trails that lead to impressive waterfalls. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027268114/civica-and-civica-waterfall-circular-along-the-tajuna-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Civica and Civica waterfall - circular along the Tajuña river&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering a scenic walk along the Tajuña River and past the Civica waterfall. The Cifuentes River in Trillo is also famous for its picturesque cascades.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on hikes in Alcarria Alta?
A: Hiking in Alcarria Alta reveals a rich tapestry of natural features. You'll encounter deep valleys and gorges carved by rivers like the Tagus and Tajuña, unique karst phenomena, and limestone formations. Iconic landmarks include the 'Tetas de Viana' twin hills near Trillo, offering panoramic views, and the extensive lavender fields of Brihuega, which are particularly stunning in July.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks to explore while hiking?
A: Yes, Alcarria Alta is rich in history and culture. Many trails connect charming towns with medieval character. For example, Brihuega, known as the 'Garden of Alcarria,' features well-preserved medieval walls and the Piedra Bermeja Castle. You can also explore ancient caves and castle ruins near Mandayona, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030348491/castillo-de-jadraque-circular-from-jadraque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Jadraque&lt;/a&gt; on a circular route from Jadraque.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Alcarria Alta?
A: The trails in Alcarria Alta are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 80 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, from river valleys to rock formations, and the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and charming villages.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Alcarria Alta offers 9 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or families. These trails provide a gentle introduction to the region's beauty without significant elevation challenges. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030348491/castillo-de-jadraque-circular-from-jadraque" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Jadraque — circular from Jadraque&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 6.9 km route that allows you to enjoy the scenery at a relaxed pace.

Q: Can I combine hiking with exploring local attractions like caves or castles?
A: Absolutely. Alcarria Alta is dotted with fascinating attractions that can be integrated into your hiking plans. You can find trails near historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5855435" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mandayona Village&lt;/a&gt;, which has ancient caves and castle ruins. The region also features several castles, such as those in &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4440570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Brihuega&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5854808" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Torija&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of outdoor activity and cultural exploration.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature the famous lavender fields?
A: Yes, if you visit in July, you can experience the stunning lavender fields. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1027253632/jardin-de-la-alcarria-circular-through-the-lavender-fields-of-brihuega" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jardín de La Alcarria — circular through the lavender fields of Brihuega&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12 km route specifically designed to showcase these vibrant and aromatic landscapes around Brihuega.

Q: What are some notable viewpoints or panoramic spots in Alcarria Alta?
A: For spectacular panoramic views, consider hiking towards the 'Tetas de Viana' near Trillo. These twin hills are an emblematic landmark of Alcarria Alta, and one of them is accessible via a rustic metal staircase, rewarding you with breathtaking vistas of the surrounding region.
tips: El Cid or Jadraque Castle is a 15th century Spanish fortress located in the municipality of Jadraque (Guadalajara). From its height, it watches over an extensive area of the Henares river plain, with the mountains of northern Guadalajara in the background.
